success_surface: Cell-level text replacement with preservation of file integrity and comprehensive validation
effective_behaviors: Copy source file to output path before any modifications to preserve original data
Verify sheet availability programmatically before accessing worksheet data
Check data type before applying string transformations to avoid errors
Iterate through explicitly defined ranges matching instruction scope
Apply conditional logic to modify only cells meeting transformation criteria
Reload workbook in read-only mode after save to verify changes persisted correctly
Log detailed operation summaries including cell coordinates, before/after values, and counts
Select openpyxl for cell-level value modifications requiring format preservation
evidence: Agent used shutil.copy() to create output file before loading workbook for editing
Agent printed wb.sheetnames and confirmed 'Sheet1' exists before assignment
Agent checked isinstance(original_value, str) before calling .replace() on cell contents
Agent looped through range(1, 5) matching the A1:A4 answer position specification
Agent only replaced quotes when '"' in original_value, leaving other cells untouched
Agent reloaded workbook with read_only=True after wb.save() and verified each cell value
Agent printed summary with quotes_removed count, cells_modified list, and per-cell logs
Agent chose openpyxl.load_workbook() instead of pandas.read_excel() for direct cell manipulation

memory_items.title: File Integrity Through Pre-Copy
memory_items.lesson: Always copy the source file to the output path before making any modifications
memory_items.generalization: Create a backup or working copy at the start of manipulation tasks to ensure source files remain untouched and allow for rollback if needed

memory_items.title: Data-Type Safe Transformations
memory_items.lesson: Validate cell value types before applying string or mathematical operations
memory_items.generalization: Check that values are of the expected type (string, number, etc.) before performing operations to prevent runtime errors on mixed-content cells

memory_items.title: Post-Save Verification Pattern
memory_items.lesson: Reload the saved workbook in read-only mode to confirm changes persisted correctly
memory_items.generalization: After saving modifications, independently verify the output by reading back key cells or ranges to ensure the write operation succeeded

memory_items.title: Scope-Constrained Iteration
memory_items.lesson: Iterate only within the explicitly defined answer position range
memory_items.generalization: Bound all loops and iterations to match the specified cell ranges from instructions rather than processing entire sheets or using heuristic max dimensions

memory_items.title: Detailed Operation Logging
memory_items.lesson: Record cell coordinates, before/after values, and modification counts during execution
memory_items.generalization: Maintain comprehensive logs of what was changed to enable debugging, verification, and clear communication of results to users

edits.content:
## 6. Trace-Derived Lessons
- **Pre-Modification Copy**: Always copy the source file to the output path before beginning any edits. This protects the original data and satisfies file integrity requirements.
- **Type Validation Before Transformation**: Check cell value types (e.g., `isinstance(value, str)`) before applying string or numeric operations to handle mixed-content cells safely.
- **Read-Back Verification**: After saving changes, reload the workbook in read-only mode and spot-check affected cells to confirm modifications persisted without corruption.
- **Instruction-Bounded Loops**: Iterate only within the ranges specified by `answer_position` rather than processing full sheets or inferred dimensions.
- **Comprehensive Change Logging**: Track and report cell coordinates, before/after values, and modification statistics to provide transparent audit trails.
